title: Tellicherry Fort
tagline: Tellicherry Fort — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Tellicherry Fort is a fortress located in Thalassery (Tellicherry), a town in North Malabar of Kerala state, South India.
overview.description: Tellicherry Fort is a fortress located in Thalassery (Tellicherry), a town in North Malabar of Kerala state, South India. Thalassery was one of the most important European trading centers in Kerala. The Fort lies on a group of low wooden hills running down to the sea and is protected by natural waters. It has been the main outlet for the rich spices, hill products, and timber of the vast hinterland.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thalassery
topPlaces.description: Thalassery (Malayalam: [t̪ɐlɐʃ(ː)eːɾi] ) (also called Tellicherry) is a city and municipality on the Malabar Coast in Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India, bordered by the districts of Mahe and Kozhikode. Thalassery municipality has a population of just under 100,000 as of 2011 census.

topPlaces.name: Overbury's Folly
topPlaces.description: Overbury's Folly is a seaside park with a watch tower. It is located less than a kilometer from Thalassery, a commercial town on the Malabar Coast in the Kannur district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thalassery Stadium
topPlaces.description: Thalassery Stadium (also known as Thalassery Municipal Cricket Ground) is located near the Thalassery Civil Station, Kerala state, South India. Thalassery Stadium hosts the Ranji Trophy cricket matches every year.

topPlaces.name: Odathil Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Odathil Mosque, also known as the Odathil Palli, is a mosque, located in the city of Thalassery, in the state of Kerala, India. The mosque was built in c. 1806. .

topPlaces.name: Siege of Tellicherry
topPlaces.description: The siege of Tellicherry was a military embargo that happened in Thalassery (North Malabar). The Commander in Chief of the Mysore Calicut Province, Sirdar Ali, took siege of the British Military Barrack of Thalassery for 18 months.

topPlaces.name: Thalassery Pier
topPlaces.description: Thalassery Pier, locally known as Kadalpalam, is located in Thalassery, Kannur District of Kerala state, South India. It is an old pier extending out into the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Sree Sundareswara Temple
topPlaces.description: The Sree Sundareswara Temple is one of two prominent temples consecrated by Sree Narayana Guru Devan in Kannur District, the other being Sree Jagannaatha Temple, Thalassery. The temple is located in suburban Talap in the Kannur district of Kerala, India, which is approximately 2 km away from Kannur Railway Station, and 3 km from Kannur New Bus Stand, Thavakkara.

topPlaces.name: Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Temple, Thalassery
topPlaces.description: Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Temple is a Hindu temple associated with Gowda Saraswatha Brahmins (GSB) in and around Thalassery. The temple is located in Thalassery town, in the Kannur district of Kerala.

items.note: Thalassery (Malayalam: [t̪ɐlɐʃ(ː)eːɾi] ) (also called Tellicherry) is a city and municipality on the Malabar Coast in Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India, bordered by the districts of Mahe and Kozhikode. Thalassery municipality has a population of just under 100,000 as of 2011 census.
Overbury's Folly is a seaside park with a watch tower. It is located less than a kilometer from Thalassery, a commercial town on the Malabar Coast in the Kannur district, Kerala, India.

Thalassery Stadium (also known as Thalassery Municipal Cricket Ground) is located near the Thalassery Civil Station, Kerala state, South India. Thalassery Stadium hosts the Ranji Trophy cricket matches every year.
The Odathil Mosque, also known as the Odathil Palli, is a mosque, located in the city of Thalassery, in the state of Kerala, India. The mosque was built in c. 1806. .

The siege of Tellicherry was a military embargo that happened in Thalassery (North Malabar). The Commander in Chief of the Mysore Calicut Province, Sirdar Ali, took siege of the British Military Barrack of Thalassery for 18 months.
Thalassery Pier, locally known as Kadalpalam, is located in Thalassery, Kannur District of Kerala state, South India. It is an old pier extending out into the Arabian Sea.

The Sree Sundareswara Temple is one of two prominent temples consecrated by Sree Narayana Guru Devan in Kannur District, the other being Sree Jagannaatha Temple, Thalassery. The temple is located in suburban Talap in the Kannur district of Kerala, India, which is approximately 2 km away from Kannur Railway Station, and 3 km from Kannur New Bus Stand, Thavakkara.
Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Temple is a Hindu temple associated with Gowda Saraswatha Brahmins (GSB) in and around Thalassery. The temple is located in Thalassery town, in the Kannur district of Kerala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tellicherry Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tellicherry Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tellicherry Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (85 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (140 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tellicherry Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tellicherry Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tellicherry Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Tellicherry Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tellicherry Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thalassery, Overbury's Folly, Thalassery Stadium, Odathil Mosque, Siege of Tellicherry.
